name: Breakfast Risotto
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine apple juice, cinnamon sticks and nutmeg. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat; immediately reduce heat to low and keep warm.
2. In a separate saucepan, warm milk over medium-low heat. Turn off heat and keep warm.
3. In a large, non-aluminum saucepan over medium-low heat, melt butter. Add rice and salt and stir with a wooden spoon until the rice begins to turn translucent, about 2 to 3 minutes.
4. Ladle about half a cup of hot apple juice and stir the rice until it absorbs the juice. Continue adding juice about one-quarter cup at a time until absorbed.
5. When all the juice has been absorbed, ladle in about half a cup of warm milk along with raisins. Stir until the milk is absorbed.
6. Add remaining milk the same way, stirring after each addition until fully absorbed before adding more milk.
7. All liquid will have been added and rice will be tender in about 30 minutes. Test a few kernels; they should be al dente - creamy on the outside but firm to the bite at the center.
8. Remove pan from heat, cover and let stand for about 5 minutes. Serve risotto into warmed individual bowls. Pass brown sugar and milk or half-and-half at the table to be added to taste.
4 servings, excluding optional toppings, each provides 503 calories (7.8g fat or 13.8% of total). Original recipe used whole milk.
